title: henckelia oblongifolia
intro: Henckelia oblongifolia, also known as the Indian gentian, is a species of flowering plant in the gentian family. It is native to the Western Ghats of India, and has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Henckelia oblongifolia include reducing fever, treating respiratory problems, and improving digestion, as well as its use as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, Henckelia oblongifolia is often used to make teas, decoctions, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
